title: ATAH Decision Explanation
description: ATAH Core Protocol object: machine-readable explanation of a single protocol decision (discovery result, exclusion, ordering choice, handoff state transition, withdrawal, suppression, or data-sharing event). Every meaningful protocol decision is explainable; per spec §11A, transparency is a top-level conformance requirement. **Design rule (important).** This schema defines the decision-explanation object DIRECTLY — i.e. the object IS the explanation. Where a response schema references this object, the field name in the consuming response is `decision_explanation`, but this schema does NOT wrap its own contents in a `decision_explanation` key. Implementers MUST NOT re-wrap when copying or referencing; doing so produces a double-nesting bug. The two-layer transparency model in §11A is delivered by including TWO `decision_explanation` fields in `match-response.schema.json` (one at response level, one per candidate), each a separate instance of this schema. Required for any ATAH-conformant implementation that produces or consumes responses carrying explanations.

decision_type.description: What kind of decision this explanation covers. `discovery_result` for an included candidate in a Discovery response; `exclusion` for an excluded candidate or excluded class; `ordering` for the ordering-policy decision at response level; `handoff` for a Component 2 lifecycle transition; `withdrawal` for any §11.9 withdrawal scenario; `suppression` for a partner-data-conflict or admin-suppression event; `data_sharing` for vault retrieval or contact-detail release.

rules_applied.description: Rule identifiers covering the rules a candidate or action passed (or, for `exclusion`, failed). v0.8.2 ships with the following canonical initial set; the enum is extensible through governance review and grows as the protocol matures. Initial v0.8.2 set: `category_match`, `jurisdiction_match`, `matching_status_active`, `compliance_status_active`, `contact_verified`, `availability_match`, `verification_threshold_met`, `category_qualification_match`, `commercial_weighting_excluded`, `band_assignment_applied`, `professional_delegated_authority_present` (professional acting under delegated authority), `consent_receipt_continuity_verified` (Component 2 consumption), `step_up_authentication_completed` (high-impact withdrawal). Implementations MAY extend with additional rule identifiers; the field is open so v0.8.3 / v0.9 can add canonical values without a breaking schema change.

ordering_policy.description: The ordering policy applied to this decision. For `decision_type: discovery_result` and `ordering`, this mirrors the response-level `presentation_disclosure.ordering_policy` from `match-response.schema.json`. For `decision_type: exclusion`, the policy is recorded for traceability (the candidate would have been ordered under this policy if not excluded). For other decision types, the field MAY be omitted or carry only `commercial_weighting: false`.

mode.description: Mirrors the nine Phase 5 ordering modes plus `not_applicable` for non-ordering decision types.

authority_basis.description: The credential class under which the decision was taken. Single source of truth lives in `principal-delegation.schema.json#/$defs/AuthorityBasis`. For consumer-mediated decisions, `user_session`. For professional-side actions, `professional_delegated_token`. For partner / verifier actions, `partner_credential`. For state-changing introduction calls, `handoff_access_token`. For admin actions, `governance_admin_role`. For internal state-machine events, `system_role`.

audit_event_id.description: Identifier of the corresponding audit event recorded for this decision (`audit-event.schema.json` `event_id`). Lets governance / audit retrieval cross-reference the decision-explanation object back to the audit log entry. Per the §11A transparency-as-conformance rule, every recorded decision-explanation MUST link to its audit event.

exclusion_reason_category.description: For `decision_type: exclusion` only. The reason category for the exclusion (e.g. `jurisdiction_mismatch`, `matching_status_inactive`, `category_not_active`, `contact_unverified`, `profile_incomplete`, `verification_threshold_not_met`, `compliance_pending`). Aggregated reason-category counts appear in the response-level `exclusion_summary` on `match-response.schema.json`. Named excluded candidates are not surfaced to consumers; only governance/auditor retrieval (`GET /v1/decision-explanations/{audit_event_id}`) returns the per-candidate detail.

withholding_basis.description: Per §11A, privacy / security / anti-gaming reasons MAY justify withholding details from the explanation. When a detail is withheld, this field records the basis. The reason something happened MUST still be explainable at category level even when specific details are withheld; `no_withholding` is the default and indicates full disclosure of the explanation fields.
